title: rubus orthacanthus subsp. euorthacanthus
intro: Rubus orthacanthus subsp. euorthacanthus, also known as the wild bramble or thorny raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus orthacanthus plant, which is a type of wild raspberry native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Asia. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus orthacanthus subsp. euorthacanthus include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and boost the immune system.
